content: 80% of the world's population is deficient in magnesium, highlighting a widespread nutritional issue.
George Eie's study showed that 2 to 500 mg of magnesium per meal reduced symptoms of chronic stress, anxiety, depression, and addiction.
Magnesium sits on the ndma receptor, guarding it from triggering stress responses, and preventing the release of neurotransmitters like epinephrine and norepinephrine.
Without magnesium, the ndma receptor can be easily triggered, leading to increased stress and cortisol production, which can damage the hippocampus and impair stress regulation.
Taking magnesium supplements can break the cycle of chronic stress and improve sleep patterns, motivation, and overall health.
Historically, people in Paleolithic times had adequate magnesium intake from nutrient-rich soil, seafood, and meats, allowing their bodies to recover from stress more effectively.
